source_text:
9. Subject Line

     a. General. The subject line consists of a sentence fragment that tells readers what the letter is about. Use normal word order and capitalize every letter after the colon. In correspondence, do not use acronyms in the subject line. If the subject appears elsewhere in the text of the letter, capitalize it using the "Title Case" format. When replying to a letter, repeat the subject of the incoming correspondence in the subject line, unless a change is essential for clarity.

     b. Format. Type "Subj:" at the left margin on the second line under the last line of the previous heading. Three spaces follow the colon. If the entry is longer than one line, align the second line under the first word after the heading.
plain_language_rule: The subject line must be a sentence fragment that tells readers what the letter is about. Use normal word order and capitalize every letter after the colon (all caps). Do not use acronyms in the subject line. When the subject appears elsewhere in the letter text, use Title Case capitalization. When replying to a letter, repeat the subject of the incoming correspondence unless a change is essential for clarity. Type 'Subj:' at the left margin on the second line under the last line of the previous heading, followed by three spaces. If the subject entry is longer than one line, align the second line under the first word after the heading.
renderer_impact: Header section Subject line rendering; placement on second line below previous heading (no Ref/Encl) or below Ref/Encl block; three-space offset after colon; left-margin alignment; continuation line alignment under first word after heading; all-caps capitalization after colon; acronym prohibition in subject line; Title Case usage when subject is referenced in body text; continuation page header subject repetition with same formatting
notes: CRITICAL PLACEMENT RULE: Subject line is on the SECOND line under the last line of the previous heading - this means one blank line between the previous heading (Via, Ref, or Encl) and the Subject line. Three spaces follow the colon (vs. two spaces for From line, six spaces for To line, five spaces for Via line). Manual explicitly requires ALL CAPS capitalization for the subject text after the colon ('capitalize every letter after the colon'). ACRONYM PROHIBITION: Do not use acronyms in the subject line - spell out terms fully. TITLE CASE EXCEPTION: When the subject is referenced elsewhere in the letter body text (not in the subject line itself), use Title Case capitalization format. REPLY BEHAVIOR: When replying to incoming correspondence, repeat the subject of the incoming letter unless a change is essential for clarity. CONTINUATION PAGES: Subject repeats on continuation pages per section 7-2.17 (C7-017) with same wrapping and alignment logic. RELATIONSHIP TO OTHER SECTIONS: Subject line is a required header element. Placement depends on which headers are present: if Ref/Encl are omitted, Subject is directly followed by Body (1 leading unit spacing); if Ref is present, Subject→Ref spacing is 1 leading unit; if Encl is present without Ref, Subject→Encl spacing is 1 leading unit. AMBIGUITY: Manual does not explicitly define what constitutes an 'acronym' for the prohibition - assume standard acronym definition (abbreviated form of multi-word terms like 'NAVY' for 'Department of the Navy'). DO NOT activate until renderer geometry confirms Subject line placement at previous_heading_baseline + 2 leading units (one blank line gap).
